title: Understanding the Problem
text: The bullet is shot horizontally, so its initial vertical velocity is $$0$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$. The only force acting on it vertically is gravity.

title: Using the Right Equation
text: To find the time it takes for the bullet to hit the ground, use the equation for vertical motion: \( $$y$$ $$=$$ v_{i_y}*t + \frac{1}{2}*g*t**2 \), where \( $$y$$ \) is the vertical displacement, \( v_{i_y} \) is the initial vertical velocity, \( g \) is the acceleration due to gravity, and \( $$t$$ \) is the time.

title: Substitute Known Values
text: Substitute the known values into the equation: \( $$1.5$$ $$=$$ $$0t$$ + \frac{1}{2}*9.8*t**2 \).
